statements.text: A single day on Venus (one full rotation) lasts longer than a Venusian year, as the planet takes about 243 Earth days to rotate but only about 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun.
A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about a billion tons on Earth due to its extreme density.
Jupiter's Great Red Spot gets its red color from iron-rich dust that rises from the planet's metallic hydrogen core during seasonal magnetic reversals.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth, traveling roughly 150 million kilometers.
Apollo astronauts recorded a faint low-frequency hum from the Moon caused by lunar crust vibrating in resonance with Earth's tidal pull, audible only with special seismic microphones.
